query: IOC International Biathlon Union Ski Snowboard Federation neutral flag Russian athletes 2026 Winter Games qualification
a_issues: Includes Wikipedia, which is less authoritative than IOC/FIS primary sources. Result set is sparse and does not directly include the International Biathlon Union or FIS qualification ruling details from primary sources. Wikipedia snippet may contain future-dated or hard-to-verify claims and is not ideal for grounding. However, it does include an official IOC result stating AINs compete at Milano Cortina 2026 under the same conditions as Paris 2024.
b_issues: Top result is a Facebook post, which is weak and incomplete. CBS/Moscow Times snippets say Russian athletes are allowed under a neutral flag, but do not provide the detailed IOC conditions or federation-specific qualification context. One result from The Inertia says Russian and Belarusian athletes will not be allowed to compete in qualifying events for 2026 as neutral athletes, which conflicts with the anchor and later FIS/CAS developments. The set is more contradictory and relies more on secondary/non-authoritative sources.
reasoning: A better enables a correct answer because it contains a direct IOC source stating that Individual Neutral Athletes with Russian or Belarusian passports can compete at Milano Cortina 2026 under the same conditions as Paris 2024, which is central to the query about the IOC, neutral status/flag, and Russian athletes. B has some relevant snippets, but it is noisier and internally less reliable: the Facebook/CBS snippets are thin, and The Inertia result contradicts the later CAS/FIS position reflected in the anchor. Neither set cleanly covers the International Biathlon Union piece, but A has the stronger authoritative grounding and fewer contradictions.
